name: Tokushima Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the verdant mountains and natural abundance of Tokushima
purity, harmony and the bright future of the prefecture

description: A green field charged at the centre with a white emblem combining the katakana ト and ク of the prefecture's name.
history: Tokushima's prefectural symbol was adopted on 11 September 1966. The flag, with the white emblem on a green field, has been in use as the prefectural flag since the same year.

features.symbolism: the lush mountains and forests of Shikoku and the prefecture's natural abundance

features.role: Tokushima prefectural emblem (stylised katakana トク)
features.symbolism: a stylised arrangement of the katakana ト (to) and ク (ku) of 徳島 (Tokushima), forming a balanced symbol representing the development and unity of the prefecture.
